content.full_text:
California
• Fair Political
Practices Commission
August 24,
1993
Christine Peirson Baker
Senior Vice President
Independence One Bank
of California
26722 Plaza Drive, Suite 120
Mission Viejo,
CA
92691
RE:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1-93-284
Dear Ms.
Baker:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)
Your letter dated July 14,
1993 correctly summarizes my
advice.
The Act does not impose limitations on contributions to
state ballot measure committees.
These contributions may also be
made directly from corporate funds.
1
!
However,
an individual or
entity that makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a
calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or committees
becomes a major donor committee and must file Oampaign disclosure
statements.
(Section 82013.)
I have enclosed “Campaign Information Manual E” which
provides reporting requirements for major donor committees.
If you
have any questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—
5662.
Sincerely,
IT
t/ifJ1J
Linda Moureaux
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
1!
The Commission’s jurisdiction is limited to the
provisions of the Political Reform Act.
You may also wish to
contact the Federal Election Commission to determine if there are
any federal restrictions on contributions by federally chartered
institutions.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: The Act does not impose limitations on contributions to state ballot measure committees. These contributions may also be made directly from corporate funds. However, an individual or entity that makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or committees becomes a major donor committee and must file campaign disclosure statements.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign contribution limitations and disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act for contributions to state ballot measure committees and major donor committees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: State ballot measure contributions have no contribution limits and may come from corporate funds, but any entity contributing $10,000 or more in a calendar year to candidates or committees must register and file as a major donor committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter of prior telephone advice rather than a response to a written request with formal Q/C sections. The question and conclusion are synthesized from the letter's body. Footnote 1 notes jurisdictional limitations regarding federal law.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign contribution limitations and disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act for contributions to state ballot measure committees and major donor committees?

CONCLUSION: The Act does not impose limitations on contributions to state ballot measure committees. These contributions may also be made directly from corporate funds. However, an individual or entity that makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or committees becomes a major donor committee and must file campaign disclosure statements.
embedding.first_500_words: California • Fair Political Practices Commission August 24, 1993 Christine Peirson Baker Senior Vice President Independence One Bank of California 26722 Plaza Drive, Suite 120 Mission Viejo, CA 92691 RE: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice Our File No. 1-93-284 Dear Ms. Baker: You have requested confirmation of telephone advice concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) Your letter dated July 14, 1993 correctly summarizes my advice. The Act does not impose limitations on contributions to state ballot measure committees. These contributions may also be made directly from corporate funds. 1 ! However, an individual or entity that makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to or at the behest of candidates or committees becomes a major donor committee and must file Oampaign disclosure statements. (Section 82013.) I have enclosed “Campaign Information Manual E” which provides reporting requirements for major donor committees. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322— 5662. Sincerely, IT t/ifJ1J Linda Moureaux Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division Enclosure 1! The Commission’s jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Political Reform Act. You may also wish to contact the Federal Election Commission to determine if there are any federal restrictions on contributions by federally chartered institutions. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC confirmation of telephone advice regarding campaign contribution limitations under the Political Reform Act. The letter confirms that no contribution limits apply to state ballot measure committees (including corporate contributions), but contributions of $10,000+ annually to candidates/committees trigger major donor committee registration and reporting requirements.
